title: Architecture Variants (Encoder-Only, Decoder-Only, Encoder-Decoder)
summary: Three transformer variants. Encoder-only (BERT): bidirectional, understanding. Decoder-only (GPT): causal, generation. Encoder-decoder (T5): sequence-to-sequence. Selection depends on task structure.
key_insights: Encoder-only: bidirectional, no generation. Classification, retrieval, similarity, anomaly detection
Decoder-only: causal, generation by continuation. Conversation and story completion are architecturally identical
Encoder-decoder: distinct input→output transformation. Translation, summarization
Decision tree: (1) Generate? No → encoder-only. (2) Distinct I/O? Yes → enc-dec. (3) Same stream → dec-only
Encoder-only = best for knowledge storage/retrieval (richest bidirectional representation)

mathematical_core.decoder_only: $$P(x_t | x_{<t}) = \text{softmax}(\text{Decoder}(x_{<t}))$$ next token prediction, causal mask always applied
mathematical_core.encoder_decoder: $$P(y_t | y_{<t}, X) = \text{softmax}(\text{Decoder}(y_{<t}, \text{Encoder}(X)))$$ conditioned on encoded input

derivations.title: Bidirectional vs causal — information-theoretic perspective

steps.name: Encoder (bidirectional)
steps.math: Token $i$'s representation $h_i = f(x_1, \ldots, x_n)$ — conditioned on ALL tokens. Maximum mutual information $I(h_i; X)$.

steps.name: Decoder (causal)
steps.math: Token $i$'s representation $h_i = f(x_1, \ldots, x_i)$ — conditioned only on past. $I(h_i; X) \leq I(h_i; x_{1:i}) < I(h_i; x_{1:n})$. Strictly less.

steps.name: Consequence
steps.math: For understanding tasks (need max info about input): encoder-only is strictly better. For generation: causal constraint is necessary (can't condition on future).

derivations.consequence: The bidirectional/causal choice is a constraint imposed by the task, not a quality tradeoff.

derivations.title: Parameter efficiency comparison
derivations.math: For same budget $P$: encoder-only uses all $P$ for understanding. Decoder-only uses all $P$ for generation. Encoder-decoder splits: $\approx 40\%$ encoder + $\approx 53\%$ decoder + $\approx 7\%$ embeddings.
derivations.consequence: For tasks needing ONLY understanding or ONLY generation, a single-stack model uses parameters more efficiently than encoder-decoder.

related: FOUNDATIONS.INDUCTIVE_BIAS — variant selection = choosing inductive bias for task
